completed_tasks.name: Remove canvas-meta overlay (formula + heavy atom count shown top-left of Ketcher)
Remove overflow:hidden from global .ketcher (confirmed dead CSS — no element has literal class 'ketcher')
Remove overflow:hidden from global .canvas-wrap and module .canvasWrap override

remaining_tasks.name: Diagnose why Ketcher menus are still hidden — need browser DevTools inspection
Fix Ketcher menus once root cause is known

blockers.description: Cannot determine root cause of hidden Ketcher menus without live browser DOM inspection
blockers.workaround: User must open DevTools, trigger a context menu (right-click on canvas), inspect element with class ContextMenu-module_contextMenu__oqyL- and report computed top/left/z-index/display/visibility

human_actions_pending.action: Open browser DevTools → right-click Ketcher canvas → find element class=ContextMenu-module_contextMenu__oqyL- → report computed style: top, left, z-index, display, visibility, opacity
human_actions_pending.context: All overflow:hidden removed from our CSS wrappers but menus still not visible. Need to know if rendering off-screen, behind elements, or not rendering at all.

decisions.decision: Global .ketcher CSS in styles.css (display:grid etc.) is dead CSS — no element in the DOM has literal class 'ketcher'. Ketcher uses 'Ketcher-root' (capital K) as its root class name.
decisions.rationale: KETCHER_ROOT_NODE_CLASS_NAME = 'Ketcher-root' in Ketcher bundle. Exhaustive search found no literal 'ketcher' className assignment.

decisions.decision: Ketcher toolbar dropdown portals: position:absolute z-index:20, appended to .Ketcher-root. Context menu: position:fixed z-index:1. Modal overlays: position:absolute z-index:40 within .Ketcher-root.
decisions.rationale: Sourced from Ketcher built CSS: ToolbarMultiToolItem-module_portal__2XJgz and ContextMenu-module_contextMenu__oqyL-

decisions.decision: App-module_app__8cgyF has container-type:size (=contain:size layout style, NOT paint). Does NOT clip overflow, does NOT create containing block for fixed elements.
decisions.rationale: CSS Containment spec: only paint containment clips overflow and creates containing block for fixed. container-type:size excludes paint.

next_action: Ask user to provide browser DevTools inspection result for a hidden Ketcher menu element, then diagnose and fix based on actual computed styles.
context_notes: Full session analysis: removed canvas-meta overlay (user request), then attempted to fix Ketcher menu clipping. Removed overflow:hidden from all our CSS wrapper layers. After exhaustive static analysis of Ketcher bundle internals, could not determine why menus remain hidden. Strongest remaining hypotheses: (1) context menu z-index:1 is too low and something in our page creates a stacking context above it, (2) portal position calculation goes wrong because of some layout quirk, (3) something in Ketcher's own CSS I missed. The global .ketcher CSS block in styles.css is dead code that could be cleaned up but is not causing the issue.
